Military Technology: Macedonian vs Persian Warfare

The innovations that conquered an empire

Spear/Sarissa

M

Macedonian: Sarissa

18ft (5.5m)

Two-handed pike requiring both hands to wield

P

Persian: Short Spear

6-8ft (2-2.5m)

One-handed thrusting weapon used with shield

Formation

M

Macedonian: Phalanx

16x16 Grid

Massive rectangular formation with overlapping spears

P

Persian: Line Formation

Single Rank

Extended line with gaps between units

Armor

M

Macedonian: Bronze Muscle Cuirass

Heavy Bronze

Solid bronze breastplate molded to the body

P

Persian: Scale Armor & Linen Corselet

Scale + Linen

Bronze/Iron Scales

Bronze/iron scales on leather, or multi-layered linen armor

Siege

M

Macedonian: Torsion Catapults

60lb Stones

Revolutionary siege engines developed by engineers

P

Persian: Battering Rams

Basic Rams

Traditional ramming devices on wheels

Cavalry

M

Macedonian: Companion Cavalry

Heavy Shock

Elite heavy cavalry for decisive charges

P

Persian: Light Cavalry

Skirmishers

Fast, mobile archers and harassers

Innovation Statistics

18ft

Sarissa Length

20

Years of Development

0

Alexander's Inventions

Key Insight

Alexander's genius lay not in inventing new technologies, but in perfectly combining existing innovations. The sarissa, developed by his father Philip II, combined with torsion artillery and heavy cavalry to create an unstoppable military machine. Philip spent 20 years developing these systems before Alexander ever took command.
